1.grammarTitleMn: "To Be" үйл үг — am / is / are
1.grammarExplanationEn:
The verb "to be" is the MOST important verb in English — it appears in almost every sentence. In Mongolian, you can say "Би оюутан" (I student) without a verb, but in English this is WRONG. You MUST always use a verb: "I am a student." It has three present forms: am (only with I), is (he/she/it), are (you/we/they). Used for: name · job · nationality · location · age · feelings.

Subject forms:
I am — positive: I am happy. | negative: I am not sad. | question: Am I right?
You are — positive: You are kind. | negative: You are not late. | question: Are you ready?
He is — positive: He is a doctor. | negative: He is not here. | question: Is he your friend?
She is — positive: She is a teacher. | negative: She is not busy. | question: Is she a student?
It is — positive: It is cold today. | negative: It is not far. | question: Is it open?
We are — positive: We are students. | negative: We are not tired. | question: Are we late?
They are — positive: They are friends. | negative: They are not here. | question: Are they ready?

Short Forms (Contractions): I'm · You're · He's · She's · It's · We're · They're — Use in everyday speech & informal writing.

Rule 1 — Negative: Add NOT after the verb. I am not late. She is not a teacher. They are not ready.
Rule 2 — Question: Move verb to FRONT. Are you a student? Is he here? Am I right?

Grammar Tip: Use contractions in speech: I'm, She's, They're. 'am not' cannot be shortened — NEVER say 'amn't'.

Common Mistakes: He are my friend. ✅ He is. | I is happy. ✅ I am happy. | She am a doctor. ✅ She is. | They is here. ✅ They are.
1.grammarExplanationMn:
"To be" үйл үг нь англи хэлний ХАМГИЙН ЧУХАЛ үйл үг — бараг бүх өгүүлбэрт хэрэглэгддэг. Монголоор "Би оюутан" гэж болох ч англиар БУРУУ. Заавал үйл үг хэрэгтэй. Одоо цагт 3 хэлбэр: am (I), is (he/she/it), are (you/we/they).

Товчилсон хэлбэр: I'm · You're · He's · She's · It's · We're · They're — Өдөр тутмын яриа, бичвэрт хэрэглэнэ.

Дүрэм 1 — Үгүйсгэл: Үйл үгийн дараа NOT нэм. I am not late. She is not a teacher. They are not ready.
Дүрэм 2 — Асуулт: Үйл үгийг өгүүлбэрийн ЭХЭНД тавь. Are you a student? Is he here? Am I right?

Зөвлөгөө: Яриандаа товчилсон хэлбэр ашигла. 'am not'-г товчилж болохгүй — 'amn't' гэж хэлж болохгүй.

Нийтлэг алдаа: He are my friend. ✅ He is. | I is happy. ✅ I am happy. | She am a doctor. ✅ She is. | They is here. ✅ They are.

2.grammarTitleEn: Present Simple — I / You / We / They
2.grammarTitleMn: Одоо цагийн энгийн хэлбэр — I / You / We / They
2.grammarExplanationEn:
The Present Simple tense describes things we do regularly, habits, and daily routines. For I / You / We / They — just use the BASE VERB with NO changes. Add 'do not' (don't) for negatives and 'do' to make questions. Signal words: every day, always, usually, never, sometimes, on Mondays.

Rule 1 — Negative: Add DO NOT / DON'T before the verb. I don't eat meat. They don't wake up early.
Rule 2 — Question: Move DO to the front. Do you eat breakfast? Do they go to school? What time do you sleep?

Short Forms (Contractions): don't = do not. I don't · You don't · We don't · They don't — Use 'don't' in everyday speech — much more natural!

Common Mistakes:
❌ I wakes up early. ✅ I wake up early.
❌ She don't like it. ✅ She doesn't like it.
❌ Do he work here? ✅ Does he work here?
❌ I am go to school. ✅ I go to school.
2.grammarExplanationMn:
Одоо цагийн энгийн хэлбэр нь өдөр тутмын үйл явдал, дадал зуршлыг тодорхойлдог. I / You / We / They-тай үйл үгийг хэвийн хэлбэрээр ашигладаг. Үгүйсгэлд 'don't', асуултад 'do' ашигладаг. Гол үгс: every day, always, usually, never.

Үгүйсгэл: Үйл үгийн өмнө DO NOT / DON'T нэм. I don't eat meat. They don't wake up early.
Асуулт: DO-г өгүүлбэрийн эхэнд тавь. Do you eat breakfast? Do they go to school? What time do you sleep?

Товчилсон хэлбэр: don't = do not. I don't · You don't · We don't · They don't — Хэвийн яриандаа 'don't' хэрэглэ — илүү байгалийн.

Нийтлэг алдаа:
❌ I wakes up early. ✅ I wake up early.
❌ She don't like it. ✅ She doesn't like it.
❌ Do he work here? ✅ Does he work here?
❌ I am go to school. ✅ I go to school.

3.grammarTitleEn: Possessive Adjectives — my / your / his / her / our / their
3.grammarTitleMn: Өмчийн тэмдэг нэр — my / your / his / her / our / their
3.grammarExplanationEn:
Possessive adjectives show who something belongs to. They come BEFORE nouns and NEVER change based on the noun — only based on who the owner is. Key rule: 'his' for male owners, 'her' for female owners. In Mongolian: миний, чиний, түүний (эр), түүний (эм), манай, тэдний.

Subject / Possessive Adjective / Example / Question form / Mongolian:
I → my → my mother → Is this your book? → Миний
You → your → your father → Where is your bag? → Чиний / Таны
He → his → his sister → Is this his car? → Түүний (эр)
She → her → her brother → What is her name? → Түүний (эм)
We → our → our family → This is our house. → Манай
They → their → their children → Where is their dog? → Тэдний

Short Forms (Contractions): his / her / their — NO contractions! Always write these in FULL.

Rule 1 — Negative: Use NOT with 'to be': My sister is NOT old. She is young. This is NOT his book. It is HER book.
Rule 2 — Question: Is this YOUR book? What is HER name? How old is THEIR son? Whose book is this?

Grammar Tip:
• his = male owner · her = female owner
• our = we own it · their = they own it
• Монгол: миний→my · чиний→your · түүний (эр)→his · түүний (эм)→her

Common Mistakes:
❌ He is my brother. Her name is Bat. ✅ His name is Bat.
❌ She is my sister. His name is Ana. ✅ Her name is Ana.
❌ This is her book. (about a man) ✅ This is his book.
❌ My family loves their house. ✅ My family loves our house.
3.grammarExplanationMn:
Өмчийн тэмдэг нэр нь ямар нэг зүйл хэнийх болохыг харуулдаг. Нэрийн ӨМНӨ ирж, нэрний дагуу өөрчлөгддөггүй — зөвхөн эзэмшигчийн дагуу өөрчлөгддөг. Дүрэм: эр эзэмшигчид 'his', эм эзэмшигчид 'her'.

Эзэмшигч / Өмчийн тэмдэг нэр / Жишээ / Монгол:
I → my → my mother → Миний
You → your → your father → Чиний / Таны
He → his → his sister → Түүний (эр)
She → her → her brother → Түүний (эм)
We → our → our family → Манай
They → their → their children → Тэдний

Товчилсон хэлбэр: his / her / their — товчилдоггүй! Эдгээрийг үргэлж бүрэн хэлбэрт бич.

Дүрэм 1 — Үгүйсгэл: 'to be'-тэй NOT хэрэглэ: My sister is NOT old. She is young. This is NOT his book. It is HER book.
Дүрэм 2 — Асуулт: Is this YOUR book? What is HER name? How old is THEIR son? Whose book is this?

Зөвлөгөө:
• his = эр эзэмшигч · her = эм эзэмшигч
• our = бид эзэмшдэг · their = тэд эзэмшдэг
• Монгол: миний→my · чиний→your · түүний (эр)→his · түүний (эм)→her

Нийтлэг алдаа:
❌ He is my brother. Her name is Bat. ✅ His name is Bat.
❌ She is my sister. His name is Ana. ✅ Her name is Ana.
❌ This is her book. (эрийн тухай) ✅ This is his book.
❌ My family loves their house. ✅ My family loves our house.

4.grammarTitleEn: Adjectives — position and usage before nouns
4.grammarTitleMn: Тэмдэг нэр — нэрийн өмнөх байрлал ба хэрэглээ
4.grammarExplanationEn: Adjectives ALWAYS come BEFORE the noun in English: 'a red shirt' ✅ NOT 'a shirt red' ❌. Unlike Mongolian, adjectives NEVER change their form — they are the same for singular/plural, male/female. Order of multiple adjectives: Opinion → Size → Color → Noun ('a beautiful big red bag'). Short Forms (Contractions): it's = it is · 'It's blue.' = 'It is blue.' · that's = that is · 'That's beautiful!' = 'That is beautiful!' Use it's and that's in normal speech — it sounds natural. Negatives: The shirt is NOT red. It is blue. Her hair is NOT short. It is long. My bag is NOT small. Questions: What color is your shirt? Is your bag big or small? How long is her hair? Are his shoes new? Grammar Tip — Adjective order: Opinion → Size → Color → Noun. 'A beautiful big red bag' ✅ · 'A red big beautiful bag' ❌. In A1: adjective ALWAYS before noun! Common Mistakes: ❌ A dress beautiful. ✅ A beautiful dress. ❌ Hair long black. ✅ Long black hair. ❌ The shirt is blue new. ✅ The shirt is new and blue. ❌ She is beauty. ✅ She is beautiful.
4.grammarExplanationMn: Тэмдэг нэр нь англи хэлэнд үргэлж нэрийн ӨМНӨ ирдэг: 'a red shirt' ✅ 'a shirt red' ❌. Монголоос ялгаатай нь тэмдэг нэр хэзээ ч өөрчлөгддөггүй. Хэд хэдэн тэмдэг нэр дараалал: дүгнэлт → хэмжээ → өнгө → нэр. Товчилсон хэлбэр: it's = it is, that's = that is — хэвийн яриандаа it's, that's хэрэглэ — байгалийн сонсогдоно. 'It is' → 'It's' гэж товчилдог. Үгүйсгэл: The shirt is NOT red. It is blue. Her hair is NOT short. It is long. My bag is NOT small. Асуулт: What color is your shirt? Is your bag big or small? How long is her hair? Are his shoes new? Зөвлөгөө: тэмдэг нэрийн дараалал — дүгнэлт → хэмжээ → өнгө → нэр. Нийтлэг алдаа: ❌ A dress beautiful. ✅ A beautiful dress. ❌ Hair long black. ✅ Long black hair. ❌ The shirt is blue new. ✅ The shirt is new and blue. ❌ She is beauty. ✅ She is beautiful.

5.grammarTitleEn: Present Simple — He / She / It (adds -s or -es)
5.grammarTitleMn: Present Simple — He / She / It (-s эсвэл -es нэмэх)
5.grammarExplanationEn: For He / She / It in Present Simple, ADD -s or -es to the verb. Rules: most verbs add -s (eats, drinks, likes). Verbs ending in -ch, -sh, -x, -o add -es (watches, does). Verbs ending in consonant+y change y→ies (studies, tries). For negatives use DOESN'T + base verb. For questions use DOES + subject + base verb. Short forms: doesn't = does not. He doesn't · She doesn't · It doesn't. Don't (I/You/We/They) ≠ Doesn't (He/She/It) — do not mix these two! Rule 1 — Negative: He DOESN'T eat fish. She DOESN'T drink coffee. It DOESN'T cost much. (base verb after doesn't!) Rule 2 — Question: DOES he eat meat? DOES she like tea? What DOES she eat? Where DOES he go after school?
5.grammarExplanationMn: He / She / It-тай Present Simple-д үйл үгийн төгсгөлд -s эсвэл -es нэмдэг. Дүрэм: ихэнх үйл үгэнд -s нэм. -ch, -sh, -x, -o-оор төгссөнд -es нэм. Гийгүүлэгч+y→ies болгоно. Үгүйсгэлд DOESN'T + үндсэн үйлүг. Асуултад DOES + субьект + үндсэн үйлүг. Товчилсон хэлбэр: doesn't = does not. He doesn't · She doesn't · It doesn't. Don't (I/You/We/They) ≠ Doesn't (He/She/It) — энэ хоёрыг хольж хэрэглэхгүй! Үгүйсгэл: He DOESN'T eat fish. She DOESN'T drink coffee. It DOESN'T cost much. (doesn't-ийн дараа үндсэн үйлүг хэрэглэ!) Асуулт: DOES he eat meat? DOES she like tea? What DOES she eat? Where DOES he go after school?

6.grammarTitleEn: There is / There are — describing what exists in a place
6.grammarTitleMn: There is / There are — газарт юу байгааг тайлбарлах
6.grammarExplanationEn: 'There is' describes ONE thing that exists. 'There are' describes MULTIPLE things. Used to describe places, give directions, and talk about neighborhoods. Short form: There's = There is. Negative: There isn't (singular) / There aren't (plural). Question: Is there? / Are there? Remember: THERE IS/ARE describes existence, not location! Short Forms (Contractions): There's = There is · There isn't = There is not · There aren't = There are not. There's — use always in normal speech. There isn't/aren't — use in negatives. Negatives & Questions — Rule 1: Negative: There ISN'T a hospital near here. There AREN'T any shops on that street. There is NO bus stop. Rule 2 — Question: IS THERE a bank near here? ARE THERE any shops? How many schools ARE THERE in this area? Grammar Tip: There IS + single noun (one thing). There ARE + plural noun (many things). Short form: There's a bank here. Question: Is there a park? Are there shops? Common Mistakes: ❌ There are a school. ✅ There is a school. ❌ There is two banks. ✅ There are two banks. ❌ Is there banks? ✅ Are there banks? ❌ There have a park. ✅ There is a park.
6.grammarExplanationMn: 'There is' нэг зүйл байгааг харуулдаг. 'There are' олон зүйл байгааг харуулдаг. Газар байршил, чиглэл өгөхөд хэрэглэгддэг. Товч хэлбэр: There's. Үгүйсгэл: There isn't / There aren't. Асуулт: Is there? / Are there? Товчилсон хэлбэр: There's = There is · There isn't = There is not · There aren't = There are not. There's — хэвийн яриандаа үргэлж ашигла. There isn't/aren't — үгүйсгэлд хэрэглэнэ. Үгүйсгэл ба асуулт — Дүрэм 1: Үгүйсгэл: There ISN'T a hospital near here. There AREN'T any shops on that street. There is NO bus stop. Дүрэм 2 — Асуулт: IS THERE a bank near here? ARE THERE any shops? How many schools ARE THERE in this area? Зөвлөгөө: There IS + нэг нэр үг. There ARE + олон тооны нэр үг. Товч хэлбэр: There's a bank here. Асуулт: Is there a park? Are there shops? Нийтлэг алдаа: ❌ There are a school. ✅ There is a school. ❌ There is two banks. ✅ There are two banks. ❌ Is there banks? ✅ Are there banks? ❌ There have a park. ✅ There is a park.

vocabulary.example: I write in English every day.

7.grammarExplanationEn:
'Can' expresses ABILITY (what you are able to do) and PERMISSION (what you are allowed to do). CAN is the SAME for ALL subjects — it NEVER changes! No -s for He/She. After 'can' always use the BASE VERB — never add 'to'! Negative: cannot / can't. Question: put 'can' at the front.

Subject | Positive | Negative | Question
I can | I can swim. | I can't fly. | Can I help you?
You can | You can sing. | You can't run fast. | Can you dance?
He/She can | She can cook. | He can't swim. | Can she drive?
We/They can | We can play. | They can't come. | Can we go now?

Short Forms (Contractions): can't = cannot — I can't · You can't · He can't · She can't · We can't · They can't

Rule 1 — Negative: I CANNOT / CAN'T swim. She CAN'T come today. They CAN'T understand. (base verb after can't!)
Rule 2 — Question: CAN you swim? CAN she sing? WHAT CAN you do? CAN I open the window? (Permission)

Grammar Tips:
• Can = ability: I can run fast.
• Can = permission: Can I go to bathroom?
• Can = possibility: Can we meet tomorrow?
• After 'can': base verb only! can swim ✅ can to swim ❌

Common Mistakes:
❌ She cans sing. ✅ She can sing.
❌ I can to swim. ✅ I can swim. (no 'to'!)
❌ Can he swims? ✅ Can he swim?
❌ I cannot to dance. ✅ I can't dance.
7.grammarExplanationMn:
'Can' нь чадвар (чадах зүйл) ба зөвшөөрөл (зөвшөөрөгдсөн зүйл)-ийг илэрхийлдэг. Can нь БҮХ субьекттай ИЖИЛ — хэзээ ч өөрчлөгддөггүй! He/She-д -s нэмдэггүй! Can-ы дараа ҮРГЭЛЖ ҮНДСЭН ҮЙЛҮГ хэрэглэ — 'to' нэмдэггүй!

Товчилсон хэлбэр: can't = cannot — Бүх субьекттай ижил хэлбэртэй! He can ✅ — He cans ❌ · can't нь хэвийн яриандаа хэрэглэгддэг.

Үгүйсгэл: I CANNOT / CAN'T swim. She CAN'T come today. They CAN'T understand. (can't-ын дараа үндсэн үйл үг!)
Асуулт: CAN you swim? CAN she sing? WHAT CAN you do? CAN I open the window? (Зөвшөөрөл)

Нийтлэг алдаанууд:
❌ She cans sing. ✅ She can sing.
❌ I can to swim. ✅ I can swim.
❌ Can he swims? ✅ Can he swim?
❌ I cannot to dance. ✅ I can't dance.

8.objectiveMn: Гараг, сар, цагтай холбоотой 20 үг сур. What, When, Where, Who, Why, How ашиглан Wh- асуултуудыг сур, дадлагажуул.
8.grammarTitleEn: Wh- Questions — What / When / Where / Who / Why / How
8.grammarTitleMn: Wh- Асуултууд — What / When / Where / Who / Why / How
8.grammarExplanationEn: Wh- questions ask for specific information — NOT just yes or no. They begin with: What (юу/ямар), When (хэзээ), Where (хаана), Who (хэн), Why (яагаад), How (яаж). The question word comes FIRST, then the auxiliary verb (do/does/is/are), then the subject. Example: 'Where DO you live?' 'When DOES she wake up?' 'What IS today?' Short forms (contractions): What's = What is, Where's = Where is, When's = When is, Who's = Who is. Rule 1 — Negative: Why DON'T you come? (Because I am busy.) When ISN'T she at school? (On weekends.) What DON'T you like? Rule 2 — Question: WHAT do you eat? WHERE do you live? WHEN do you wake up? WHO is your best friend? HOW do you go to school? Common mistakes: ❌ Where you live? ✅ Where do you live? ❌ What time it is? ✅ What time is it? ❌ Who you are? ✅ Who are you? ❌ Why she is late? ✅ Why is she late?
8.grammarExplanationMn: Wh- асуултууд тодорхой мэдээлэл шаарддаг — зөвхөн тийм/үгүй биш. Асуулт үгс: What (юу), When (хэзээ), Where (хаана), Who (хэн), Why (яагаад), How (яаж). Дараалал: Асуулт үг → туслах үйл үг → субьект → үндсэн үйл үг. Товчилсон хэлбэр: What's = What is, Where's = Where is, When's = When is, Who's = Who is. Хэвийн яриандаа эдгээрийг хэрэглэдэг — цээжил! Үгүйсгэл: Why DON'T you come? When ISN'T she at school? What DON'T you like? Нийтлэг алдаанууд: ❌ Where you live? ✅ Where do you live? ❌ What time it is? ✅ What time is it? ❌ Who you are? ✅ Who are you? ❌ Why she is late? ✅ Why is she late?

9.grammarTitleEn: Prepositions of Place — in / on / under / next to / between
9.grammarTitleMn: Байршлын угтвар үгс — in / on / under / next to / between
9.grammarExplanationEn: Prepositions of place tell us WHERE something is located. The most common ones: IN = inside something enclosed (in the room, in the box). ON = on a surface (on the table, on the wall). UNDER = below something (under the bed). NEXT TO = right beside (next to the door). BETWEEN = in the middle of two things (between the chairs). Short forms (contractions): it's = it is, they're = they are, used when describing location, e.g. 'It's on the table.' 'They're in the kitchen.' Rule 1 — Negative: The book is NOT on the table. It is UNDER the table. The cat is NOT in the bedroom. It is IN the kitchen. Rule 2 — Question: WHERE is the cat? (It is on the sofa.) WHERE are my keys? (They are on the table.) Is the lamp NEXT TO the bed? Common mistakes: ❌ The book is in the table. ✅ The book is ON the table. ❌ She is on the bathroom. ✅ She is IN the bathroom. ❌ The cat is on under the bed. ✅ The cat is UNDER the bed. ❌ Sit in the chair. ✅ Sit ON the chair.
9.grammarExplanationMn: Байршлын угтвар үгс нь ямар нэг зүйл ХААНА байгааг хэлдэг. Хамгийн нийтлэг: IN = дотор (in the room). ON = дээр (on the table). UNDER = доор (under the bed). NEXT TO = хажуу, дэргэд (next to the door). BETWEEN = хооронд (between the chairs). Товчилсон хэлбэр: it's = it is, they're = they are — байршлыг тайлбарлахдаа хэрэглэ. Жишээ: 'It's on the table.' 'They're in the box.' Үгүйсгэл: The book is NOT on the table. It is UNDER the table. Асуулт: WHERE is the cat? (It is on the sofa.) Is the lamp NEXT TO the bed? Нийтлэг алдаа: ❌ The book is in the table. ✅ The book is ON the table. ❌ She is on the bathroom. ✅ She is IN the bathroom. ❌ The cat is on under the bed. ✅ The cat is UNDER the bed. ❌ Sit in the chair. ✅ Sit ON the chair.

10.grammarTitleEn: How much / How many — Countable & Uncountable nouns
10.grammarTitleMn: How much / How many — Тоолж болох ба болохгүй нэрс
10.grammarExplanationEn: 'How much' is used with UNCOUNTABLE nouns — things we cannot count individually (money, water, rice, milk, bread) — and to ask about price. 'How many' is used with COUNTABLE nouns — things we CAN count (apples, books, coins, shirts). Key test: Can you say '1 apple, 2 apples'? If yes = COUNTABLE → use 'How many'! Negatives: I don't have MUCH money. (uncountable) I don't have MANY coins. (countable) There isn't MUCH rice left. Questions: How MUCH does it cost? How MANY shirts do you have? How MUCH money do you need? How MANY people are here? 'A few' is used with countable nouns; 'a little' is used with uncountable nouns. Common mistakes: ❌ How many money? ✅ How much money? ❌ How much apples? ✅ How many apples? ❌ How much is it cost? ✅ How much does it cost? ❌ I have few money. ✅ I have a little money. 'How much is it?' is the most common shop question. Reply with 'It's...' e.g. 'It's 5000 tugrik.'
10.grammarExplanationMn: 'How much' нь тоолж болохгүй нэрсийг (мөнгө, ус, будаа, сүү, талх) болон үнийг асуухад хэрэглэнэ. 'How many' нь тоолж болох нэрсийг (алим, ном, зоос, цамц) асуухад хэрэглэнэ. Туршилт: '1 алим, 2 алим' гэж тоолж болдог уу? Тийм бол → 'How many'! Үгүйсгэл: I don't have MUCH money. (тоолшгүй) I don't have MANY coins. (тоолж болох) There isn't MUCH rice left. Асуулт: How MUCH does it cost? How MANY shirts do you have? 'A few' нь тоолж болох нэрстэй, 'a little' нь тоолж болохгүй нэрстэй хэрэглэнэ. Нийтлэг алдаа: ❌ How many money? ✅ How much money? ❌ How much apples? ✅ How many apples? ❌ How much is it cost? ✅ How much does it cost? ❌ I have few money. ✅ I have a little money. 'How much is it?' бол дэлгүүрт хамгийн нийтлэг асуулт бөгөөд 'It's 5000 tugrik.' гэж хариулна.

11.grammarTitleEn: What's the weather like? — Describing weather with It is / It's
11.grammarTitleMn: Цаг агаар ямар байна? — It is / It's-ээр цаг агаарыг тайлбарлах
11.grammarExplanationEn: To talk about weather, use 'It is + adjective': It is hot. It is cold. It is sunny. We can also use verb-ing forms: It is raining. It is snowing. To ask about weather: 'What is the weather like today?' or 'How is the weather?' or 'What is the temperature?'. Mongolia is famous for extreme weather — hot summers and very cold winters down to -40°C! Short Forms (Contractions): It's = It is · 'It's cold.' = 'It is cold.' · What's = What is · 'What's the weather like?' — use It's in normal speech always. Rule 1 — Negative: It ISN'T hot today. It is cold. The weather ISN'T nice. It is rainy and windy. It ISN'T summer yet. Rule 2 — Question: What's the weather like? Is it hot today? Is it raining? What is the temperature today? What season is it? Grammar Tips: 'What's the weather like?' is the most common question. Use 'It's + adjective' for states: It's cold. It's sunny. Use 'It's + verb-ing' for actions: It's raining. It's snowing. Seasons: Spring=warm, Summer=hot, Autumn=cool, Winter=cold. Common Mistakes: ❌ The weather is rain. ✅ It is raining. / It is rainy. ❌ It is a cold. ✅ It is cold. ❌ Weather is good. ✅ The weather is good. ❌ It is snow outside. ✅ It is snowing outside.
11.grammarExplanationMn: Цаг агаарын тухай: 'It is + тэмдэг нэр': It is hot / cold / sunny. Мөн: It is raining / snowing. Асуухдаа: 'What is the weather like today?' эсвэл 'How is the weather?' Монгол улс эрс тэс цаг агаараараа алдартай — халуун зун ба -40°C хүйтэн өвөл! Товчилсон хэлбэр: It's = It is · 'It's cold.' = 'It is cold.' · What's = What is · 'What's the weather like?' — It's-ийг хэвийн яриандаа үргэлж хэрэглэ. 'It is cold.' → 'It's cold.' маш нийтлэг. Үгүйсгэл: It ISN'T hot today. The weather ISN'T nice. It ISN'T summer yet. Асуулт: What's the weather like? Is it hot today? Is it raining? What is the temperature today? What season is it?

12.grammarTitleEn: What do you do? — Talking about jobs with a / an
12.grammarTitleMn: What do you do? — a / an-ийг мэргэжлийн нэрийн өмнө хэрэглэх
12.grammarExplanationEn: To talk about jobs, use: 'I AM A + job' for identity and 'I WORK AS A + job' for function. To ask about jobs: 'What do you do?' or 'What is your job?' or 'Where do you work?'. IMPORTANT: Use 'A' before jobs starting with consonant sounds (a teacher, a doctor) and 'AN' before jobs starting with vowel sounds (an engineer, an artist, an office worker). Short forms (contractions): I'm a teacher. / She's an engineer. / He's a doctor. Negatives: I am NOT a doctor. She is NOT an engineer. Questions: What DO you DO? (job) Where DO you WORK? (place) IS she a doctor? (yes/no) What IS his job? (information). Common mistakes: ❌ I am engineer. ✅ I am AN engineer. ❌ She work as nurse. ✅ She works AS A nurse. ❌ What you do? ✅ What do you do? ❌ He work at hospital. ✅ He works AT the hospital.
12.grammarExplanationMn: Ажил мэргэжлийн тухай: 'I am a + ажил' (өөрийгөө тодорхойлохдоо). 'I work as a + ажил' (функцийг хэлэхдээ). Асуухдаа: 'What do you do?' ЧУХАЛ: гийгүүлэгчээр эхэлсэн мэргэжлийн өмнө 'a', эгшигээр эхэлсэн мэргэжлийн өмнө 'an' хэрэглэнэ. Товчилсон хэлбэр: I'm a teacher. / She's an engineer. / He's a doctor. Үгүйсгэл: I am NOT a doctor. She is NOT an engineer. Асуулт: What DO you DO? Where DO you WORK? IS she a doctor? What IS his job? Нийтлэг алдаа: ❌ I am engineer. ✅ I am AN engineer. ❌ She work as nurse. ✅ She works AS A nurse. ❌ What you do? ✅ What do you do? ❌ He work at hospital. ✅ He works AT the hospital.

13.grammarTitleEn: How do you get to...? — Transport prepositions by / on / in
13.grammarTitleMn: Тээврийн угтвар үгс: by / on / in
13.grammarExplanationEn:
To talk about transport, use prepositions: BY + vehicle = the METHOD of transport (I go by bus / by car / by plane). ON + public transport = ON A BUS, ON A TRAIN (you board them). IN + private vehicle = IN A CAR, IN A TAXI (enclosed space). WALK = no preposition needed: 'I walk to school' or 'I go on foot'.

Rule 1 — Negative: I don't go by bus. I walk to school. She is NOT in the car. She is ON the bus. He doesn't take a taxi.
Rule 2 — Question: How do you GO TO school? How long does it TAKE? Which bus do you TAKE? Do you walk or take the bus?

Grammar Tips:
• BY = method: I go by bus / by car / by taxi
• ON = public transport: on the bus / on the train
• IN = private enclosed: in a car / in a taxi
• Walk = no 'by'! I walk. / I go on foot.

Common Mistakes:
❌ I go in bus. ✅ I go BY bus.
❌ She is by the car. ✅ She is IN the car.
❌ I take by taxi. ✅ I take A taxi. / I go by taxi.
❌ I walk by foot. ✅ I walk. / I go on foot.

Short Forms (Contractions): I'm on the bus. · She's in the car. · He's taking a taxi. Common in spoken English — use contractions!
13.grammarExplanationMn:
Тээврийн хэрэгслийн тухай угтвар үгс: BY + хэрэгсэл = аргыг хэлэхдээ (by bus, by car). ON + нийтийн тээвэр = ON A BUS, ON A TRAIN. IN + хувийн тээвэр = IN A CAR, IN A TAXI. WALK = угтвар үг хэрэггүй: 'I walk to school' эсвэл 'I go on foot'.

Дүрэм 1 — Үгүйсгэл: I don't go by bus. I walk to school. She is NOT in the car. She is ON the bus. He doesn't take a taxi.
Дүрэм 2 — Асуулт: How do you GO TO school? How long does it TAKE? Which bus do you TAKE? Do you walk or take the bus?

Зөвлөгөө:
• BY = арга: I go by bus / by car / by taxi
• ON = нийтийн тээвэр: on the bus / on the train
• IN = хувийн тээвэр: in a car / in a taxi
• Walk = 'by' хэрэггүй! I walk. / I go on foot.

Нийтлэг алдаа:
❌ I go in bus. ✅ I go BY bus.
❌ She is by the car. ✅ She is IN the car.
❌ I take by taxi. ✅ I take A taxi. / I go by taxi.
❌ I walk by foot. ✅ I walk. / I go on foot.

Товчилсон хэлбэр: I'm on the bus. — явж байх явцад хэрэглэнэ. 'How long does it take?' — 'It takes 20 minutes.' нийтлэг.

14.grammarTitleEn: Have got — I've got / She's got — possession and illness
14.grammarTitleMn: Have got — I've got / She's got — эзэмшил ба өвчин
14.grammarExplanationEn: 'Have got' means the same as 'have' — it is very common in spoken British English. For I/You/We/They: 'have got'. For He/She/It: 'has got'. Short forms: I've got, you've got, he's got, she's got, we've got, they've got. Used for possession: 'I've got a book.' AND for illness: 'I've got a headache.' / 'She's got a fever.' Rule 1 — Negative: I HAVEN'T GOT medicine at home. She HASN'T GOT a fever. They HAVEN'T GOT time to see a doctor. Rule 2 — Question: HAVE you got a headache? HAS she got a fever? What have you got? (illness) Have you got any medicine? Common Mistakes: She have got a cold. ✅ She HAS GOT a cold. I've got headache. ✅ I've got A headache. Has she got fever? ✅ Has she got A fever? I hasn't got time. ✅ I HAVEN'T got time.
14.grammarExplanationMn: 'Have got' нь 'have'-тай ижил утгатай — ярианы хэлэнд маш их хэрэглэгддэг. I/You/We/They: 'have got'. He/She/It: 'has got'. Товч хэлбэр: I've got, he's got, she's got. Эзэмшлийн тухай: 'I've got a book.' Өвчний тухай: 'I've got a headache.' 'She's got a fever.' Үгүйсгэл: I HAVEN'T GOT medicine at home. She HASN'T GOT a fever. They HAVEN'T GOT time to see a doctor. Асуулт: HAVE you got a headache? HAS she got a fever? What have you got? Have you got any medicine? Түгээмэл алдаа: She have got a cold. ✅ She HAS GOT a cold. I've got headache. ✅ I've got A headache. Has she got fever? ✅ Has she got A fever? I hasn't got time. ✅ I HAVEN'T got time.

15.grammarTitleEn: Present Continuous — I am doing / She is eating right now
15.grammarTitleMn: Одоо явагдаж буй хэлбэр — I am doing / She is eating right now
15.grammarExplanationEn: The Present Continuous (also called Present Progressive) describes actions happening RIGHT NOW, at this very moment. Form: am/is/are + verb + -ing. Compare with Present Simple: 'I study English every day.' (habit — Simple) vs 'I am studying English right now.' (happening NOW — Continuous). Signal words for Continuous: now, right now, at the moment, look!, currently, today (temporary). Short Forms (Contractions): I'm doing · She's eating · He's sleeping · We're studying · They're talking · It's raining. Negative: I am NOT eating. (I'm not eating.) She is NOT cooking. (She isn't cooking.) They are NOT sleeping. Question: ARE you eating? IS she cooking? WHAT are you doing? WHERE is he going? WHY are they talking? Spelling notes: run→running · sit→sitting · come→coming. Common Mistakes: ❌ I am eat now. ✅ I am EATING now. ❌ She is study now. ✅ She IS STUDYING now. ❌ They are play football. ✅ They are PLAYING football. ❌ I studying English. ✅ I AM studying English.
15.grammarExplanationMn: Одоо явагдаж буй хэлбэр нь ЯГ ОДОО болж буй үйлдлийг тодорхойлдог. Хэлбэр: am/is/are + үйл үг + -ing. Ялгаа: 'I study English every day.' (дадал — Simple) ≠ 'I am studying English right now.' (яг одоо — Continuous). Чухал үгс: now, right now, at the moment, look!, currently. Товчилсон хэлбэр: I'm, She's, He's, We're, They're — маш нийтлэг. Хэвийн яриандаа ҮРГЭЛЖ товчилсон хэлбэр хэрэглэ! Үгүйсгэл: I am NOT eating. She is NOT cooking. They are NOT sleeping. Асуулт: ARE you eating? IS she cooking? WHAT are you doing? WHERE is he going? WHY are they talking? Алдааны жишээ: ❌ I am eat now. ✅ I am EATING now. ❌ She is study now. ✅ She IS STUDYING now. ❌ They are play football. ✅ They are PLAYING football. ❌ I studying English. ✅ I AM studying English.

16.grammarTitleEn: How do you feel? — Adjectives for feelings + because
16.grammarTitleMn: Та яаж мэдэрч байна вэ? — Мэдрэмжийн тэмдэг нэр + because
16.grammarExplanationEn: To talk about feelings, use 'I am + adjective' or 'I feel + adjective'. To ask: 'How do you feel?' or 'How are you?' You can explain the reason using 'because': 'I am happy because today is Friday.' 'I am tired because I studied all night.' You can also use Present Continuous: 'I am feeling better now.' Some feelings: happy/sad (opposite), excited/bored (opposite), calm/nervous (opposite). Short Forms (Contractions): I'm happy · She's excited · He's nervous. I'm not sad · She isn't bored · They aren't tired. Rule 1 — Negative: I am NOT happy today. I am sad. She is NOT excited. She is nervous. He is NOT calm — he is very angry! Rule 2 — Question: How DO you FEEL? ARE you happy? WHY are you sad? WHAT makes you nervous? Is she excited about the trip? Grammar Tip: 'I am happy' = state of feeling. 'I am feeling happy' = right now, this moment. Why are you happy? → Because it is Friday! Opposites: happy↔sad · excited↔bored · calm↔nervous · proud↔ashamed. Common Mistakes: ❌ I am feel happy. ✅ I am happy. / I feel happy. ❌ She is tiredly. ✅ She is tired. ❌ I feel boredly. ✅ I feel bored. ❌ Why you are sad? ✅ Why ARE you sad?
16.grammarExplanationMn: Мэдрэмжийн тухай: 'I am + тэмдэг нэр' эсвэл 'I feel + тэмдэг нэр'. Асуухдаа: 'How do you feel?' Шалтгааныг 'because' ашиглан хэлнэ: 'I am happy because today is Friday.' 'I am tired because I studied all night.' Эсрэг утгатай мэдрэмжүүд: happy↔sad, excited↔bored, calm↔nervous. Товчилсон хэлбэр: I'm happy · She's excited · He's nervous. I'm not sad · She isn't bored · They aren't tired. 'I'm happy. — хэвийн яриандаа товчилсон хэлбэр хэрэглэ.' 'Why are you happy?' → 'Because it's Friday!' Үгүйсгэл: I am NOT happy today. I am sad. She is NOT excited. She is nervous. He is NOT calm — he is very angry! Асуулт: How DO you FEEL? ARE you happy? WHY are you sad? WHAT makes you nervous? Is she excited about the trip? Зөвлөгөө: 'I am happy' = мэдрэмжийн байдал. 'I am feeling happy' = одоо энэ мөчид. Эсрэг утгатай үгс: happy↔sad · excited↔bored · calm↔nervous · proud↔ashamed. Нийтлэг алдаа: ❌ I am feel happy. ✅ I am happy. / I feel happy. ❌ She is tiredly. ✅ She is tired. ❌ I feel boredly. ✅ I feel bored. ❌ Why you are sad? ✅ Why ARE you sad?

17.grammarTitleEn: Present Simple vs Present Continuous — key differences
17.grammarTitleMn: Present Simple vs Present Continuous — гол ялгаа
17.grammarExplanationEn: KEY DIFFERENCE: Present Simple = HABITS and ROUTINES (things that happen regularly, every day). Present Continuous = what is happening RIGHT NOW (at this moment). Signal words: Simple → every day, always, usually, never, often, on Mondays. Continuous → now, right now, at the moment, look!, currently, today (temporary). Some verbs NEVER use continuous: know, like, love, want, understand, remember, forget, need, have (possession). Negatives: I don't study on Sundays. (Simple — habit) vs I am not studying right now. (Continuous — this moment). Questions: Do you study every day? (Simple — habit) Are you studying now? (Continuous — now) What do you usually do? vs What are you doing?
17.grammarExplanationMn: ГОЛ ЯЛГАА: Present Simple = ДАДАЛ ЗУРШИЛ (өдөр бүр болдог зүйл). Present Continuous = ЯГ ОДОО болж буй зүйл. Чухал үгс: Simple → every day, always, usually. Continuous → now, at the moment, look! Зарим үйл үгс Continuous хэлбэрийг хэзээ ч хэрэглэдэггүй: know, like, love, want, understand, need. Үгүйсгэл: I don't study on Sundays. (Simple — дадал) vs I am not studying right now. (Continuous — яг одоо). Асуулт: Do you study every day? (Simple — дадал) Are you studying now? (Continuous — одоо) What do you usually do? vs What are you doing?

18.grammarTitleEn: Object Pronouns — me / him / her / us / them / it
18.grammarTitleMn: Объектын төлөөний үгс — me / him / her / us / them / it
18.grammarExplanationEn:
Object pronouns REPLACE nouns that receive the action of the verb. They come AFTER the verb or preposition. Subject pronouns (I, you, he, she, it, we, they) come BEFORE the verb. Object pronouns (me, you, him, her, it, us, them) come AFTER the verb. Examples: 'I love football.' → 'I love IT.' 'She helps Bat.' → 'She helps HIM.' 'Call me!' 'I like her.' 'We know them.'

Rule 1 — Negative: I don't like HIM. She doesn't know HER. They don't help US. He didn't call ME. We can't see THEM.
Rule 2 — Question: Can you help ME? Do you know HIM? Does she like THEM? Who helped HER? Did you call US?

Object pronouns have NO contractions — always write in full!

Grammar Tip: I → me · he → him · she → her · we → us · they → them · it → it. After verb: 'She likes HIM.' After preposition: 'Talk to HER.' 'A gift for US.'

Common Mistakes: ❌ She likes he. ✅ She likes HIM. ❌ Give it to she. ✅ Give it to HER. ❌ They know we. ✅ They know US. ❌ I love they. ✅ I love THEM.
18.grammarExplanationMn:
Объектын төлөөний үгс нь үйлийг хүлээн авч буй нэрийг орлодог — үйл үгийн ДАРАА ирдэг. Субьектын (I, he, she) үйл үгийн ӨМНӨ ирдэг. Объектын (me, him, her, us, them) үйл үгийн ДАРАА ирдэг. Жишээ: 'I love football.' → 'I love IT.' 'She helps Bat.' → 'She helps HIM.'

Үгүйсгэл: I don't like HIM. She doesn't know HER. They don't help US. He didn't call ME. We can't see THEM.
Асуулт: Can you help ME? Do you know HIM? Does she like THEM? Who helped HER? Did you call US?

Объектын төлөөний үгс товчилдоггүй! me, him, her, us, them — үргэлж бүрэн хэлбэрт бичнэ.

Алдаа: ❌ She likes he. ✅ She likes HIM. ❌ Give it to she. ✅ Give it to HER. ❌ They know we. ✅ They know US. ❌ I love they. ✅ I love THEM.

19.grammarTitleMn: Олон тооны нэр үг — дүрмийн (-s/-es) болон дүрмийн бус (child/children)
19.grammarExplanationEn:
Most English nouns form plurals by adding -S: dog→dogs, cat→cats, tree→trees. Nouns ending in -s, -x, -ch, -sh add -ES: bus→buses, box→boxes, church→churches. Nouns ending in consonant+y change y→IES: city→cities, baby→babies. IRREGULAR plurals must be memorized: child→children, man→men, woman→women, tooth→teeth, foot→feet, sheep→sheep (no change!), fish→fish (no change!), mouse→mice.

Rule 1 — Negative: There are NOT many fish in this river. There are NOT any horses here. The children are NOT playing.
Rule 2 — Question: How many dogs do you have? Are there any horses? How many CHILDREN are playing? Are these your FEET?

There's a dog. → There are dogs. (plural uses 'There are', not 'There is!')
There's one sheep. → There are many sheep. (same!)
19.grammarExplanationMn:
Ихэнх англи нэрс -S нэмж олон тоо үүсгэдэг: dog→dogs. -s, -x, -ch, -sh-оор төгссөн нэрс -ES нэмнэ: bus→buses. Гийгүүлэгч+y→IES: city→cities. ДҮРМИЙН БУС олон тооны үгсийг цээжлэх ёстой: child→children, man→men, woman→women, tooth→teeth, foot→feet, sheep→sheep (өөрчлөгддөггүй!), fish→fish.

Олон тоонд 'There are' хэрэглэнэ (There is биш!). Sheep, fish — нэгдмэл ба олон тоо ИЖИЛ хэлбэртэй!

20.grammarTitleEn: Would you like...? — Polite offers and requests
20.grammarTitleMn: Would you like...? — Эелдэг санал болгох ба хүсэлт
20.grammarExplanationEn: 'Would you like...?' is the POLITE way to offer or ask about preferences. It is much more polite than 'Do you want...?'. To ORDER food: 'I would like + noun' OR 'I'll have + noun' OR 'Can I have + noun please?'. To RESPOND to an offer: 'Yes please' or 'No thank you'. IMPORTANT: In a restaurant, NEVER say 'I want...' — always use 'I would like' or 'I'll have'. It sounds much more polite! Short Forms (Contractions): I'd like = I would like · 'I'd like a coffee.' I'll have = I will have · 'I'll have the soup.' Negatives: I would NOT like coffee. I would like tea. No thank you, I wouldn't like dessert. I don't want anything else. Questions: WOULD you like coffee? What WOULD you like? Are you READY to order? Can I HAVE the bill? WHAT do you recommend? Common Mistakes: ❌ I want coffee. ✅ I would like coffee. / I'll have coffee. ❌ Would you like to coffee? ✅ Would you like SOME coffee? ❌ Can I have a menu? ✅ Can I see THE menu? ❌ I would like to have soup. ✅ I would like the soup. / I'll have the soup.
20.grammarExplanationMn: 'Would you like...?' нь санал болгох, асуухад хэрэглэдэг эелдэг хэлбэр. 'Do you want...?'-аас илүү зохистой. Захиалга өгөхдөө: 'I would like + нэр' эсвэл 'I'll have + нэр'. Хариу өгөхдөө: 'Yes please' эсвэл 'No thank you'. ЧУХАЛ: Ресторанд 'I want...' гэж ХЭЛЭХГҮЙ — үргэлж 'I would like' хэрэглэ! Товчилсон хэлбэр: I'd like = I would like · 'I'd like a coffee.' I'll have = I will have · 'I'll have the soup.' Үгүйсгэл: I would NOT like coffee. No thank you, I wouldn't like dessert. Асуулт: WOULD you like coffee? What WOULD you like? Are you READY to order? Can I HAVE the bill? WHAT do you recommend? Нийтлэг алдаа: ❌ I want coffee. ✅ I would like coffee. ❌ Would you like to coffee? ✅ Would you like SOME coffee? ❌ Can I have a menu? ✅ Can I see THE menu? ❌ I would like to have soup. ✅ I would like the soup.

21.grammarTitleEn: Imperatives — Do / Don't / Let's — commands and suggestions
21.grammarTitleMn: Тушаалын хэлбэр — Do / Don't / Let's — тушаал ба санал
21.grammarExplanationEn: Imperatives are commands, instructions, requests, and suggestions. They use the BASE VERB with NO subject. Positive: just use the verb → 'Open the book.' 'Listen carefully.' Negative: Don't + verb → 'Don't run!' 'Don't be late!' Suggestion: Let's + verb → 'Let's study together!' 'Let's go!' Polite: add 'please' → 'Please sit down.' 'Don't be late, please.' Used in recipes, instructions, safety rules, directions, and everyday requests. Short forms: Don't = Do not · 'Don't run!' · 'Don't be late!' Let's = Let us · 'Let's go!' · 'Let's study!' Common mistakes: ❌ You open the door. ✅ Open the door! ❌ Don't to run! ✅ Don't run! ❌ Let's to study. ✅ Let's study. ❌ Please you sit down. ✅ Please sit down.
21.grammarExplanationMn: Тушаалын хэлбэр нь тушаал, зааварчилгаа, хүсэлт, саналд хэрэглэгддэг. Субьектгүй, зөвхөн ҮНДСЭН ҮЙЛҮГ. Эерэг: үйл үг + → 'Open the book.' Үгүйсгэл: Don't + үйл үг → 'Don't run!' Санал: Let's + үйл үг → 'Let's study together!' Зохистой: 'please' нэм → 'Please sit down.' Don't = Do not — хэвийн яриандаа үргэлж хэрэглэнэ! Let's = Let us — санал болгохдоо хэрэглэнэ. Маш нийтлэг! Нийтлэг алдаа: ❌ You open the door. ✅ Open the door! ❌ Don't to run! ✅ Don't run! ❌ Let's to study. ✅ Let's study. ❌ Please you sit down. ✅ Please sit down.

vocabulary.example: Three days ago I was sick.

22.grammarTitleEn: Past Simple — was / were (To Be in the past)
22.grammarTitleMn: Өнгөрсөн цаг — was / were ('To Be' үйл үгийн өнгөрсөн цаг)
22.grammarExplanationEn: Past Simple of 'To Be' has two forms: WAS (for I, he, she, it — singular) and WERE (for you, we, they — plural). WAS and WERE are the past tense of IS/AM and ARE. Use them to describe states, feelings, locations, and conditions in the past. Negative: was not = wasn't / were not = weren't. Question: move WAS/WERE to the front of the sentence. Rule 1 — Negative: I WASN'T at school yesterday. She WASN'T happy. They WEREN'T ready. We WEREN'T late — we were early! Rule 2 — Question: WAS she at school? WERE they happy? WHERE were you yesterday? WHY was he late? WHAT was the weather like? Common Mistakes: ❌ I were sick. ✅ I WAS sick. ❌ They was late. ✅ They WERE late. ❌ Was they there? ✅ WERE they there? ❌ She were happy. ✅ She WAS happy.
22.grammarExplanationMn: 'To Be' үйл үгийн өнгөрсөн цаг: WAS (I, he, she, it — нэгдмэл) ба WERE (you, we, they — олон тоо). WAS/WERE нь IS/AM ба ARE-ийн өнгөрсөн цаг. Өнгөрсөн цагийн байдал, нөхцөл, байршлыг тодорхойлоход хэрэглэнэ. Үгүйсгэл: wasn't / weren't. Асуулт: WAS/WERE-г эхэнд тав. Алдаа: ❌ I were sick. ✅ I WAS sick. ❌ They was late. ✅ They WERE late. ❌ Was they there? ✅ WERE they there? ❌ She were happy. ✅ She WAS happy.

vocabulary.example: She watched a movie last night.

23.grammarTitleMn: Өнгөрсөн цаг — ердийн үйл үгс (-ed нэмэлт)
23.grammarExplanationEn: Regular verbs form the Past Simple by adding -ED to the base verb. Rules: 1) Most verbs: just add -ed → walk→walked, talk→talked. 2) Verbs ending in -e: add only -d → arrive→arrived, love→loved. 3) Verbs ending in consonant+y: change y→ied → study→studied, try→tried. 4) Short verbs with vowel+consonant: double the last letter → stop→stopped, plan→planned. For NEGATIVES use DIDN'T + base verb. For QUESTIONS use DID + subject + base verb.
23.grammarExplanationMn: Ердийн үйл үгс өнгөрсөн цагт -ED нэмдэг. Дүрэм: 1) Ихэнх үйл үгэнд -ed нэм → walk→walked. 2) -e-ээр төгссөнд -d нэм → arrive→arrived. 3) Гийгүүлэгч+y→ied болгоно → study→studied. 4) Богино үйл үгийн эцсийн гийгүүлэгчийг давтана → stop→stopped. Үгүйсгэлд DIDN'T + үндсэн үйл үг. Асуултад DID + субьект + үндсэн үйл үг.

24.grammarTitleEn: Past Simple — irregular verbs (go/went, eat/ate, see/saw)
24.grammarTitleMn: Өнгөрсөн цаг — тэгдэггүй үйл үгс (go/went, eat/ate, see/saw)
24.grammarExplanationEn:
Many common English verbs have IRREGULAR past tense forms — they do NOT follow the -ed rule and must be memorized! Common patterns: go→went, eat→ate, see→saw, have→had, take→took, give→gave, come→came. KEY RULE for negatives and questions: ALWAYS use DID + BASE VERB, never the irregular past form. 'She didn't GO.' ✅ NOT 'She didn't went.' ❌ 'Did you SEE?' ✅ NOT 'Did you saw?' ❌

Rule 1 — Negative: She DIDN'T GO to school. (NOT 'didn't went') I DIDN'T SEE the movie. They DIDN'T HAVE enough time.
Rule 2 — Question: DID you GO? DID she EAT? WHAT DID you SEE? WHERE DID they GO? DID you HAVE a good time?

Short Forms (Contractions): didn't = did not (same as regular verbs!)
I didn't go. · She didn't eat. · Did you see?

Common Mistakes:
❌ She goed to school. ✅ She WENT to school.
❌ I eated lunch. ✅ I ATE lunch.
❌ Did you saw the movie? ✅ Did you SEE the movie?
❌ She didn't went. ✅ She didn't GO.

Top 10 irregular verbs to memorize: go/went · eat/ate · see/saw · have/had · do/did · come/came · take/took · give/gave · say/said · make/made
NEVER add -ed to irregular verbs: ❌ goed, eated, sawed
24.grammarExplanationMn:
Олон нийтлэг үйл үгс -ed дүрмийг дагадаггүй — дүрмийн бус өнгөрсөн цагийн хэлбэртэй. Цээжлэх ёстой! Нийтлэг хэлбэрүүд: go→went, eat→ate, see→saw, have→had. ГОЛ ДҮРЭМ: Үгүйсгэл, асуултад ҮРГЭЛЖ DID + ҮНДСЭН ҮЙЛҮГ: 'She didn't GO.' ✅ 'She didn't went.' ❌

Тэгдэггүй үйл үгс ч гэсэн didn't / did ашигладаг!
I didn't go. = I did not go. — үндсэн үйл үг (went биш!)

Нийтлэг алдаа:
❌ She goed to school. ✅ She WENT to school.
❌ I eated lunch. ✅ I ATE lunch.
❌ Did you saw the movie? ✅ Did you SEE the movie?
❌ She didn't went. ✅ She didn't GO.

25.grammarTitleEn: Past Simple — questions and negatives with did / didn't
25.grammarTitleMn: Өнгөрсөн цаг — did / didn't ашиглан асуулт, үгүйсгэл
25.grammarExplanationEn: To make QUESTIONS in Past Simple: DID + subject + BASE VERB + ? → 'Did you go?' 'Did she eat?' 'What did he do?' To make NEGATIVES: subject + DIDN'T + BASE VERB → 'I didn't go.' 'She didn't eat.' CRITICAL RULE: After DID or DIDN'T, ALWAYS use the BASE VERB — NEVER the past form! 'Did you went?' ❌ → 'Did you GO?' ✅ 'I didn't ate.' ❌ → 'I didn't EAT.' ✅ Rule 1 — Negative: I DIDN'T GO yesterday. (not 'didn't went') She DIDN'T EAT lunch. (not 'didn't ate') They DIDN'T COME. Rule 2 — Question: DID + subject + BASE VERB? Did you GO? Did she EAT? What DID you DO? Where DID they GO? Short Forms: didn't = did not · 'I didn't go.' Did you? — NO contraction for questions with 'did'. Common Mistakes: ❌ Did you went to school? ✅ Did you GO to school? ❌ She didn't ate. ✅ She didn't EAT. ❌ I not go yesterday. ✅ I DIDN'T go yesterday. ❌ You did go? ✅ DID you go?
25.grammarExplanationMn: Өнгөрсөн цагийн АСУУЛТ: DID + субьект + ҮНДСЭН ҮЙЛҮГ? → 'Did you go?' 'What did he do?' ҮГҮЙСГЭЛ: субьект + DIDN'T + ҮНДСЭН ҮЙЛҮГ → 'I didn't go.' ЧУХАЛ ДҮРЭМ: DID/DIDN'T-ийн дараа ҮРГЭЛЖ ҮНДСЭН ҮЙЛҮГ — өнгөрсөн цагийн хэлбэрийг ХЭЗЭЭ Ч хэрэглэдэггүй! 'Did you went?' ❌ 'Did you GO?' ✅ Товчилсон хэлбэр: didn't = did not · 'I didn't go.' 'Did you...?' — асуултыг товчилдоггүй. Шууд 'Did' хэрэглэнэ. Нийтлэг алдаа: ❌ Did you went to school? ✅ Did you GO to school? ❌ She didn't ate. ✅ She didn't EAT. ❌ I not go yesterday. ✅ I DIDN'T go yesterday. ❌ You did go? ✅ DID you go?

26.grammarTitleEn: Going to — plans and intentions for the future
26.grammarTitleMn: Going to — ирээдүйн төлөвлөгөө ба санаа
26.grammarExplanationEn: 'Going to' is used to talk about PLANS and INTENTIONS — things you have already decided to do in the future. Form: am/is/are + going to + BASE VERB. 'I am going to study tomorrow.' 'She is going to travel next summer.' 'They are going to visit us next week.' For negatives: am/is/are + NOT + going to + BASE VERB. For questions: am/is/are + subject + going to + BASE VERB? Short forms (contractions): I'm going to · She's going to · He's going to · We're going to · They're going to. 'gonna' = very informal spoken form of 'going to' — never write it! Signal words: tomorrow, next week, next year, soon.
26.grammarExplanationMn: 'Going to' нь ирээдүйд хийхээр ШИЙДСЭН ЗҮЙЛИЙН ТӨЛӨВЛӨГӨӨ, санаануудын тухай ярихад хэрэглэгддэг. Хэлбэр: am/is/are + going to + ҮНДСЭН ҮЙЛҮГ. 'I am going to study tomorrow.' Үгүйсгэл: am/is/are + NOT + going to + үндсэн үйлүг. Асуулт: am/is/are + субьект + going to + үндсэн үйлүг? Товчилсон хэлбэр: I'm going to · She's going to · He's going to · We're going to · They're going to. 'gonna' нь зөвхөн хэвийн ярианд хэрэглэгддэг — бичихгүй! Цаг заасан үгс: tomorrow, next week, next year, soon.

27.grammarTitleEn: Would like to / Want to — polite requests and desires
27.grammarTitleMn: Would like to / Want to — эелдэг хүсэлт ба хүсэл
27.grammarExplanationEn: 'Would like to' is the POLITE form of 'want to'. Use it for wishes, requests, and invitations in formal or polite situations. 'I would like to make an appointment.' 'She would like to meet on Friday.' 'Would you like to come?' Short form: 'I'd like to'. 'Want to' is less formal: 'I want to meet you.' Use 'would like to' in professional or polite situations — it always sounds more respectful! Short Forms (Contractions): I'd like to = I would like to. She'd like to = She would like to. I wouldn't = I would not. Negatives: I WOULDN'T LIKE TO cancel our appointment. She DOESN'T WANT TO meet on Monday. Questions: WOULD you LIKE TO meet? WHEN would you like to come? WHAT TIME is convenient? HOW ABOUT Thursday? Common Mistakes: ❌ I would like meet you. ✅ I would like TO meet you. ❌ Would you like to comes? ✅ Would you like to COME? ❌ I'd like to an appointment. ✅ I'd like TO MAKE an appointment. ❌ She want to meet. ✅ She WANTS to meet. (3rd person -s!)
27.grammarExplanationMn: 'Would like to' нь 'want to'-ийн ЭЕЛДЭГ хэлбэр. Хүсэл, хүсэлт, урилгад хэрэглэнэ. 'I would like to make an appointment.' Товч хэлбэр: 'I'd like to'. 'Want to' нь бага зэрэг бүдүүлэг: 'I want to meet.' Албан ёсны болон зохистой нөхцөлд 'would like to' хэрэглэ — эелдэг сонсогдоно! Товчилсон хэлбэр: I'd like to = I would like to. She'd like to = She would like to. I wouldn't = I would not. Үгүйсгэл: I WOULDN'T LIKE TO cancel our appointment. She DOESN'T WANT TO meet on Monday. Асуулт: WOULD you LIKE TO meet? WHEN would you like to come? WHAT TIME is convenient? HOW ABOUT Thursday? Нийтлэг алдаанууд: ❌ I would like meet you. ✅ I would like TO meet you. ❌ Would you like to comes? ✅ Would you like to COME? ❌ I'd like to an appointment. ✅ I'd like TO MAKE an appointment. ❌ She want to meet. ✅ She WANTS to meet. (3-р биед -s!)

28.grammarTitleEn: Mixed tenses review — Past / Present / Future together
28.grammarTitleMn: Бүх цагийн давталт — Өнгөрсөн / Одоо / Ирээдүй хамтад нь
28.grammarExplanationEn:
In real conversations, we mix ALL tenses together. PAST tells what happened: 'I went to Korea last year.' PRESENT describes now: 'I am studying English now.' FUTURE shows plans: 'I am going to travel next summer.' Using all three tenses together is what real fluency looks like! Key signal words: PAST → yesterday/last year/ago. PRESENT → now/every day. FUTURE → tomorrow/next week/going to.

Negatives & Questions:
Rule 1 — Negative: I DIDN'T travel last year. (past) I AM NOT traveling now. (present cont.) I AM NOT GOING TO go next month. (future)
Rule 2 — Question: DID you travel? (past) Do you travel often? (habit) Are you traveling now? (present cont.) Are you going to travel? (future plan)

Grammar Tips:
• Past: I went / I was / I studied (+ed or irregular)
• Present Simple: I go / I study (habits)
• Present Continuous: I am going / I am studying (now!)
• Going to: I am going to go / study (future plan)

Common Mistakes:
❌ Yesterday I go to school. ✅ Yesterday I WENT to school. (past!)
❌ Now I study. ✅ Now I AM STUDYING. (continuous!)
❌ I am going to went. ✅ I am going to GO. (base verb!)
❌ Last year I am happy. ✅ Last year I WAS happy. (past!)
28.grammarExplanationMn:
Өдөр тутмын яриандаа бид БҮХ цагийг хольж хэрэглэдэг. ӨНГӨРСӨН — болсон зүйл: 'I went to Korea.' ОДОО — одоо болж буй зүйл: 'I am studying.' ИРЭЭДҮЙ — төлөвлөгөө: 'I am going to travel.' Чухал үгс: ӨНГӨРСӨН → yesterday, last year, ago. ОДОО → now, every day. ИРЭЭДҮЙ → tomorrow, next week, going to.

Үгүйсгэл ба асуулт:
Дүрэм 1 — Үгүйсгэл: I DIDN'T travel last year. (өнгөрсөн) I AM NOT traveling now. (одоо) I AM NOT GOING TO go next month. (ирээдүй)
Дүрэм 2 — Асуулт: DID you travel? (өнгөрсөн) Do you travel often? (зуршил) Are you traveling now? (одоо) Are you going to travel? (ирээдүйн төлөвлөгөө)

Бүх цагийг хамтад нь хэрэглэж болно! 'Last year I went, now I'm studying, next year I'm going to travel!' — энэ бол жинхэнэ яриа!

29.grammarTitleEn: All Grammar Review — am/is/are · can · Simple · Past · Going to
29.grammarTitleMn: Бүх дүрмийн давталт — am/is/are · can · Simple · Past · Going to
29.grammarExplanationEn:
Today we review ALL grammar from 28 days! A1 covers: To Be (am/is/are/was/were) · Present Simple (habits: I go, she goes) · Present Continuous (now: I am going) · Can/Can't (ability) · There is/There are · Past Simple (I went, I walked) · Going to (future plans) · Possessive adjectives (my/your/his/her) · Prepositions (in/on/under) · Object pronouns (me/him/her/us/them). You have learned ALL of these — every single one!

Short Forms (Contractions): I'm · You're · He's · She's · It's · We're · They're · can't · didn't · wasn't · weren't · don't · doesn't · I'm going to. (I'm=I am · can't=cannot · didn't=did not · wasn't=was not)

Negatives & Questions:
Rule 1 — Negative: Present: I don't go. She doesn't. · Past: I didn't go. · To Be past: wasn't. weren't. · Can: I can't. · Going to: I'm not going to.
Rule 2 — Question: Present: Do you? Does she? · Past: Did you? · To Be: Are you? Was she? Were they? · Can: Can you? · Going to: Are you going to?

Grammar Tip: PAST → yesterday/last week/ago + was/went · PRESENT → every day/now + study/am studying · FUTURE → tomorrow/next week + am going to · After can/can't/did/didn't → ALWAYS base verb!

Common Mistakes: ❌ I am go every day. ✅ I GO every day. (habit=Simple) · ❌ Yesterday I go. ✅ Yesterday I WENT. (past!) · ❌ She cans swim. ✅ She CAN swim. (can never changes) · ❌ I am going to went. ✅ I am going to GO. (base verb!)
29.grammarExplanationMn:
Өнөөдөр 28 өдрийн БҮХ дүрмийг давтана! A1 түвшин: To Be · Present Simple · Present Continuous · Can/Can't · There is/are · Past Simple · Going to · Өмчийн тэмдэг нэр · Байршлын угтвар · Объектын төлөөний үг. Та БҮГДИЙГ сурсан — бүрэн бүтэн!

Товчилсон хэлбэр: I'm · You're · He's · She's · It's · We're · They're · can't · didn't · wasn't · weren't · don't · doesn't · I'm going to. Бүх товчилсон хэлбэрийг санана уу!

Үгүйсгэл ба асуулт:
Дүрэм 1 — Үгүйсгэл: Одоо: I don't go. She doesn't. · Өнгөрсөн: I didn't go. · To Be өнгөрсөн: wasn't. weren't. · Can: I can't. · Going to: I'm not going to.
Дүрэм 2 — Асуулт: Одоо: Do you? Does she? · Өнгөрсөн: Did you? · To Be: Are you? Was she? Were they? · Can: Can you? · Going to: Are you going to?

Зөвлөгөө: ӨНГӨРСӨН ЦАГ → yesterday/last week/ago + was/went · ОДОО → every day/now + study/am studying · ИРЭЭДҮЙ → tomorrow/next week + am going to · can/can't/did/didn't-ийн дараа ҮРГЭЛЖ үндсэн үйл үг!

Нийтлэг алдаанууд: ❌ I am go every day. ✅ I GO every day. · ❌ Yesterday I go. ✅ Yesterday I WENT. · ❌ She cans swim. ✅ She CAN swim. · ❌ I am going to went. ✅ I am going to GO.

30.grammarExplanationEn:
Congratulations — you completed A1! In 30 days you learned: 600 new words · To Be · Present Simple · Present Continuous · Can/Can't · There is/are · Possessive adjectives · Prepositions · Object pronouns · Past Simple regular + irregular · Going to · Would like to · Imperatives · Plurals · How much/many · Wh- questions. You can now introduce yourself, describe people and places, talk about the past, and plan for the future. You ARE A1 level English!

Grammar Summary Table:
Day 1 — To Be: I am Bat. / I am not a teacher. / Are you a student? (am/is/are/was/were)
Days 2-5 — Present Simple: I study every day. / I don't study on Sundays. / Do you study? (habits & facts)
Days 15/17 — Present Continuous: I am studying now. / I am not sleeping. / Are you studying? (right now)
Days 22-25 — Past Simple: I went / I walked. / I didn't go. / Did you go? (past actions)
Days 7/26 — Can / Going to: I can swim! / I can't fly. / Can you swim? (ability & plans)

All contractions you know: I'm · You're · He's · She's · It's · We're · They're · can't · don't · doesn't · didn't · wasn't · weren't · I'd like · I'm going to

Negatives A1 summary: am not · isn't · aren't · wasn't · weren't · don't · doesn't · didn't · can't · am not going to. You know them ALL!
Questions A1: Am/Is/Are? · Was/Were? · Do/Does? · Did? · Can? · Are going to? · What/Where/When/Who/Why/How? You know them ALL!

Your A1 toolkit is COMPLETE! ✅ Introduce yourself in English ✅ Describe people, places, things ✅ Talk about the past ✅ Make future plans ✅ Have basic conversations ✅ Read and understand simple English!

You made mistakes — that means you LEARNED! Every mistake was a step forward. You kept going even when it was hard. That courage is what makes a great English learner! 🌟

30.grammarExplanationMn:
Баяр хүргэе! Та A1 дуусгасан! 30 хоногт та: 600 шинэ үг · To Be · Present Simple · Present Continuous · Can/Can't · There is/are · Өмчийн тэмдэг нэр · Угтвар үг · Past Simple · Going to · Would like to · Тушаалын хэлбэр — БҮГДИЙГ суралцсан. Та одоо өөрийгөө танилцуулж, өнгөрсөн болон ирээдүйн тухай ярьж чаддаг. Та ЖИНХЭНЭ A1!

Та өнөөдөр БҮХ товчилсон хэлбэрийг мэднэ! Эдгээр нь жинхэнэ яриан дахь англи хэлний эрдэнэс.
30.readingPassageEn: Day 30. Graduation Day. Thirty days ago, Bat couldn't speak any English. Today is completely different. He can introduce himself, describe his family, talk about food, give directions, order at a restaurant, make appointments, and discuss his future — all in English! He studied every single day. He never gave up. Now he is going to continue to Book 2. This journey is just beginning. Congratulations, Bat — and congratulations, YOU! 🎓
speakingPrompts: Introduce yourself completely — name, age, family, job, hobbies, plans!
What was your favorite day in this book? Why?
What can you do in English now that you couldn't on Day 1?
What are you going to do to keep improving?
Tell me your best memory from the last 30 days.
What is your advice for someone starting Book 1 today?

30.listeningScriptEn: Teacher: Class, this is our final day — Graduation Day! Bat, how do you feel? Bat: I feel amazing! One month ago I couldn't speak English. Now I can! Teacher: What did you enjoy most? Bat: I loved the listening scripts and role play dialogues — they felt real! Teacher: Sarnai, what are you going to do after this? Sarnai: I am going to start Book 2 immediately! I want to reach B1 and travel to Korea! Teacher: You are both incredible! I am so proud of you! Congratulations to everyone! Everyone: Thank you, teacher! We love English! See you in Book 2! 🎓

30.homeworkEn: Writing: Write a letter to yourself — what did you achieve? What are you going to do next? Vocabulary: Teach someone 10 English words from this book — you are already a teacher! Speaking: Record yourself in English for 1 minute — listen back and celebrate how far you came! Next step: 🚀 Start Book 2 (A2 Level) — Speak English in 90 Days continues!
30.homeworkMn: Бичих: Өөртөө захидал бич — та юу хийж чадсан бэ? Дараа нь юу хийх гэж байна вэ? Үг: Энэ номноос 10 англи үгийг хэн нэгэнд заа — та аль хэдийн багш болсон! Ярих: Англиар 1 минут дуу бич — буцааж сонсоод хэр холд хүрснийгээ тэмдэглэ! Дараагийн алхам: 🚀 Ном 2-ыг (A2 түвшин) эхэл — Speak English in 90 Days үргэлжилж байна!
